intro: The biggest real split between CockroachDB and Xata is lock-in posture: CockroachDB carries low lock-in (low — postgresql-compatible, standard sql), while Xata carries medium lock-in (postgres under hood, exportable). That single fact shapes the rest of the comparison. CockroachDB is built for apps, global and is not a fit for simple, offline projects. Xata is built for apps, global and is not a fit for simple, offline projects. On the scoring side, CockroachDB rates 5/5 for developer experience and 3/5 for transparency; Xata rates 5/5 and 4/5 respectively. The honest trade-off: with CockroachDB you accept that simple key-value storage needs, while Xata comes with the reality that simple key-value storage needs. Neither side is free. CockroachDB's headline strength in the data is that apps needing managed database. Xata's headline strength is that apps needing managed database. Read each side's best-for and not-for fields carefully before you pick; the shape of your team and workflow matters more than any single score.
quick_take: CockroachDB is for apps, global; Xata is for apps, global; decide based on lock-in tolerance.
verdict_a: Choose CockroachDB if you are apps, global and can accept low lock-in (low — postgresql-compatible, standard sql).
verdict_b: Choose Xata if you are apps, global and need medium lock-in (postgres under hood, exportable).

faq.a: CockroachDB uses a hybrid model, while Xata uses a freemium model, and both publish a free tier. We don't have per-seat numbers in this dataset, so 'cheaper' depends on volume. Price both on your actual usage before deciding.

faq.q: Can I migrate from CockroachDB to Xata?
faq.a: CockroachDB has low lock-in (low — postgresql-compatible, standard sql); Xata has medium lock-in (postgres under hood, exportable). Migration effort scales with how much state and automation you've built in CockroachDB; plan for data export, config rebuild, and a parallel-run period before cutting over.
Both CockroachDB and Xata score 5/5 on developer experience in our data, so DX isn't the tiebreaker here. Decide on fit, pricing, or lock-in instead.

faq.q: Is Xata a good alternative to CockroachDB?
faq.a: Xata is built for apps, global and explicitly not for simple, offline projects, so it's a fit only if your workflow matches its stated audience. If you were using CockroachDB for apps, global, the switch is reasonable; outside that scope, look elsewhere.
